steps.title: Separate two clauses
steps.content: The sentence contains two independent clauses: the first says the situation has not always been the case, and the second says the magnetic poles have swapped places. The word though acts parenthetically between them.

steps.title: Reject broken punctuation
steps.content: Choice A places the semicolon before though, stranding the parenthetical. Choice B leaves a comma splice between independent clauses. Choice D omits the semicolon needed to separate those clauses.

steps.title: Set off though correctly
steps.content: Choice C uses a comma before though to mark the parenthetical and a semicolon after it to separate the two independent clauses. The answer is C.
